articles.title: BAE faces £120m lawsuit over decision to scrap support for aid aircraft
articles.description:
EnComm Aviation says the firm’s action has cut off vital support for crisis-hit countries including South Sudan and the DRC
Britain’s biggest weapons manufacturer, BAE Systems, is facing a £120m lawsuit after scrapping support for aircraft used to deliver aid to some of the world’s neediest countries.
EnComm Aviation, a Kenya-based aid cargo operator, claims the decision forced the cancellation of humanitarian contracts and reduced supplies to South Sudan, now threatened by famine, Somalia and the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), among others.

articles.title: Iran war may cause food shortages in Africa, world’s largest fertiliser firm says
articles.description:
Yara CEO warns of global auction that would leave poorest countries scrambling for supplies they can ill afford
The Iran war could have “dramatic consequences”, causing food shortages and price rises in some of Africa’s poorest and most vulnerable communities, the head of the world’s largest fertiliser company has said.
Svein Tore Holsether, the chief executive of Yara International, said world leaders needed to guard against soaring prices and shortages of fertiliser causing a de facto global auction that would leave the poorest countries, particularly in Africa, scrambling for supplies they could ill afford.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: How the brain recovers from noise-induced damage
articles.description: When a sound stops, our auditory system generates a precise "offset" response that marks this moment. This enables the brain to measure the duration of a sound and detect brief gaps in communication signals, such as in conversations. Researchers at LMU have now discovered how the brain is able to preserve this crucial aspect of hearing—the ability to detect when a sound ends—when it has previously been exposed to damaging noise levels.

articles.title: GLP-1 drugs have long-term benefit for heart health, review suggests
articles.description: New research has shown that GLP‑1 weight-loss drugs deliver protection against heart attacks, strokes, and premature death over a sustained period of time. Researchers from Anglia Ruskin University (ARU) analyzed data from more than 90,000 patients enrolled in large-scale international studies and found that people given glucagon-like peptide‑1 (GLP‑1) receptor agonists were significantly less likely to suffer major cardiovascular events than those given a placebo.

articles.description: In the video, the K9 can be seen barking at Allen through a doorway near metal detectors before the handler pulled the dog away as about a dozen federal officers were seen taking down magnetometers and casually standing around.

articles.title: Man charged with attempted murder after stabbings of Jewish men in London
articles.description: A 45-year-old man has been charged with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London

articles.title: Karnataka government launches India’s first digital grievance redressal system for gig workers
articles.description: The mechanism — a first of its kind in India — has been developed by the Karnataka Platform-based Gig Workers’ Board in collaboration with the Department of e-Governance

articles.title: Why the Supreme Court gutted the Voting Rights Act after six decades in a blow to Black politicians
articles.description: All six conservative justices voted to gut the Voting Rights Act in a ruling that critics say will reshape political representation for Black Americans.

articles.title: Dijo Kappan, activist who championed consumer rights, passes away
articles.description: A member of the Edamattam Kappil family of Pala, Kappan was active in student politics as a leader of the Kerala Student Congress, the campus wing of the Kerala Congress

articles.title: Election Results 2026: When will counting begin in Kerala, Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry — Where to watch
articles.description: The Election Commission of India will announce results for the 2026 Assembly elections in several states on May 4, with counting starting at 8:00 AM. Official results can be checked on the Election Commission’s website and LiveMint.

articles.title: PM Narendra Modi to visit Hyderabad on May 10, to launch ₹7,800-crore worth development initiatives in Telangana
articles.description: Prime Minister to inaugurate the third railway line between Kazipet and Vijayawada, virtually inaugurate a major petroleum products terminal at Mallapur in Hyderabad

topStories.title: Silent on Ladakhi people's demands of Statehood, Sixth Schedule status: Congress slams Amit Shah
topStories.description: “The HM is in Ladakh today basking in the glory of the Piprahwa relics, while remaining silent on the demands of the people there for Statehood, Sixth Schedule status, and protection of land and employment," Jairam Ramesh said on X.

topStories.title: Two Kentucky bank employees shot and killed during robbery, police hunting suspect
topStories.description: Police are searching for a suspect after two U.S. Bank employees were shot and killed during a robbery at the Berea, Kentucky branch on Thursday.

topStories.description: Making his first appearance before Congress since the Trump administration went to war against Iran, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth faced withering questioning from skeptical Democrats.

topStories.description: "We've been warning about this for a long time," one local resident told CBS News. "It's like a tsunami — you see the smaller waves before the big one hits."

topStories.title: U.K. raises terrorism threat level to "severe" after stabbing attack
topStories.description: The U.K. has raised its national threat level from "substantial" to "severe," citing the increasing threat of Islamist and extreme right-wing terrorism in the country.

topStories.description: The project aims to build an immediate buffer against critical mineral supply shocks while using future purchases to send a stronger demand signal to US and friendly-nation producers.

dailyNews.title: "It's Been 2 Hours": Supriya Sule, Stuck In Traffic, Talks To Motorists
dailyNews.description: Nationalist Congress Party (Sharad Pawar) Supriya Sule leader shared a video of the long traffic jam, with motorists around her interacting with her.

dailyNews.title: Gold falls around 2% in one week as skyrocketing oil prices fuel inflation worries. What lies ahead?
dailyNews.description: Gold prices are nearly flat, but poised for a weekly loss as soaring oil prices fuel inflation fears and expectations of prolonged higher interest rates.  The ongoing Iran-US conflict is keeping markets on edge, with analysts predicting continued volatility.  However, long-term forecasts suggest significant gold price appreciation.

dailyNews.title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents' dinner
dailyNews.description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities say a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents' Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

dailyNews.title: Artemis crew says they wanted to 'connect with humanity,' show what can be done when they put their mind to it
dailyNews.description: The Artemis II crew spoke with U.S. Ambassador to the U.N. Mike Waltz after their historic 10-day lunar flyby, calling the record-breaking mission a glorious experience.

dailyNews.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 63 as Trump signals possible attacks
dailyNews.description: Iran’s president calls the US siege 'intolerable' as Donald Trump says war may resume.

dailyNews.title: 2 employees dead in Kentucky bank robbery, search for suspect continues: State police
dailyNews.description: A masked man shot and killed two employees during a bank robbery in central Kentucky on Thursday afternoon and the search for the suspect continues, authorities said.

dailyNews.title: Zelenskyy says he's seeking details of Putin's May 9 ceasefire proposal
dailyNews.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is seeking details of a short-term ceasefire Russia proposed to U.S. President Donald Trump.

dailyNews.title: China’s EV price war turns into AI arms race beyond cheaper cars
dailyNews.description: Chinese EV firms are rapidly rolling out in-car AI features to gain an edge, but as the price war drags on, those technologies are quickly becoming commoditized.

dailyNews.title: Oil Companies’ Huge Profits Revive Calls for Temporary Windfall Taxes
dailyNews.description: European nations imposed temporary taxes in the 2022 energy shock when Russia invaded Ukraine, but whether they can effectively help households is up for debate.

dailyNews.title: Oil rises as White House says Iran ceasefire halts 60-day war deadline
dailyNews.description: Under the 1973 War Powers Resolution, a U.S. president needs to withdraw troops 60 days after reporting their deployment to Congress

dailyNews.title: Camp Mystic withdraws application for 2026 camp license, will not reopen
dailyNews.description: This comes on the heels of the first public hearing over last summer's Fourth of July floods that killed more than 100 people across the Hill Country, including 27 girls at Camp Mystic.

dailyNews.title: Video captures massive explosion as NYPD officers approach home, 1 dead
dailyNews.description: A body believed to be that of the suspect was recovered after an explosion triggered a five-alarm fire at a home in Queens early Thursday morning.

topStories.description: The Department of Justice released new video that shows the suspect in the White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting running through the security checkpoint and appearing to point his weapon at a Secret Service agent. NBC News’ Kelly O’Donnell breaks down the new images.

topStories.description: More state and federal approvals are needed for the 3-foot-wide Bridger Pipeline Expansion, which would stretch from the Canadian border with Montana down through eastern Montana and Wyoming, where it would link up with another pipeline.

topStories.description: Newly released Justice Department footage appears to show the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect rushing a security checkpoint armed.

dailyNews.title: ‘The Apprentice’ reboot buzz builds as Trump says Don Jr has ‘charisma’ for the role
dailyNews.description: President Trump addressed rumors "The Apprentice" could return with Donald Trump Jr. as host, saying his son would "be probably good" for it.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s Plans to Boost Weapons Production Might Not Deliver for Years
dailyNews.description: While the defense industry has announced plans to make more munitions, much of that expanded production will not quickly kick in.

dailyNews.title: Comey Indictment Shows Justice Dept. Got the Message From Bondi’s Firing
dailyNews.description: In naming only an interim successor as acting attorney general, President Trump has established even greater incentives to execute his most extreme demands, current and former officials say.

dailyNews.title: Decoding the King: Brits Hear Subtle Rebuke to Trump that Americans Might Miss
dailyNews.description: Royal watchers in Britain called the visit of King Charles III to America a master class in understated criticism.

dailyNews.title: Last on King Charles’s U.S. Tour: A Potluck and a Win for Scottish Whisky
dailyNews.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla had a ceremonial farewell at the White House before attending a block party in Front Royal, Va.

dailyNews.title: As Iran war nears key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
dailyNews.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

dailyNews.title: New White House drug abuse strategy floats wastewater testing, AI, more treatment
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is proposing wastewater testing to try to ferret out data on illegal drug use in real time, according to a draft of a new drug control strategy obtained by CBS News. It also proposes using AI to track threats.

dailyNews.title: Breaking down OPEC and its influence as UAE decides to leave
dailyNews.description: President Trump gave his endorsement to the United Arab Emirates' decision to leave the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, or OPEC, on Wednesday. "The Daily Report" breaks it down.

dailyNews.title: CBS News' Fin Gómez reflects on correspondents' dinner shooting: "Incredible force of reporters"
dailyNews.description: CBS News political director and executive director of politics and White House Fin Gómez reflects on the White House Correspondents' Dinner attack on "The Daily Report," saying he was proud of "our fellow colleagues."

dailyNews.title: "It's amazing how resilient consumer spending has been" amid the war, analyst says
dailyNews.description: The national average gas price is up to $4.30 per gallon, the highest in four years, as President Trump said Thursday that gas prices "will drop like a rock" when the Iran war ends. Ted Rossman, principal analyst at Bankrate, joins "The Daily Report" with more.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs bill funding DHS, ending record-breaking 76-day shutdown
dailyNews.description: The longest shutdown of a federal department in U.S. history came to an end on Thursday when President Trump signed a bill to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security following a breakthrough on Capitol Hill.

dailyNews.title: Confrontation between officers and alleged shooter at D.C. gala lasted 7 seconds
dailyNews.description: Seven seconds passed between when the alleged gunman at Saturday's White House Correspondents' Dinner — carrying a shotgun initially concealed by a jacket — first encountered federal law enforcement and when he was subdued, sources told CBS News.
